observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Monitor and demand timely submission of liquidation reports and/or immediate refund of any unused balances from accountable officers as soon as the purpose of the cash advance has been served; and

Submit the quarterly reports on the status of unliquidated cash advances within the reglementary period as required under PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to timely submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and

The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly project status reports of various government PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Implement alternative procedures to gather relevant information and evidence to support the reported prior years’ balances such as, but not limited to, obtaining third-party confirmations in order to reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers;

Provide appropriate disclosures in the financial statements regarding the limitations and uncertainties associated with the prior years’ balances; and




Make the necessary correcting entries to accurately reflect the receipt of fund transfer from the National Housing Authority.
We recommended that the Municipal Assessor’s Office follow-up the titling of all parcels of land of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the requirements on the grant and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with existing rules and regulations.  Send demand letters to officials and employees requiring liquidation of their outstanding cash advances. If liquidation was not made despite several demand letters sent, management may consider legal action as one of the options in collecting the unliquidated cash advance.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare appropriate adjusting entries to correct the balance reported in the CIP account and the corresponding PPE accounts; and
Recognize the depreciation expense and the corresponding accumulated depreciation of the affected PPE account.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant ensure the timely transfer of unexpended balances of the current year’s LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund at year-end in compliance with Section 21 of RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002, to prevent misstatement of accounts and safeguard the funds exclusively for disaster risk reduction and management activities.

We recommended and the Management agreed to cancel checks long outstanding and revert the amounts to the unappropriated surplus of the fund and adjust its books for fair presentation of Cash in Bank, Accounts Payable and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements pursuant to the provisions of Section 59 of the  Manual for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Implement alternative procedures to gather relevant information and evidence to support the reported prior years’ balances such as, but not limited to, obtaining third-party confirmations in order to reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers;
Provide appropriate disclosures in the financial statements regarding the limitations and uncertainties associated with the prior years’ balances; and
Ensure that all future transactions are properly supported with detailed subsidiary records and reconciled regularly to the general ledger to enhance the reliability, accuracy, and completeness of reported balances in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Acting Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant, at the beginning of the year, a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which will serve as basis of the latter for the booking up of the RPT Receivables; and
The Municipal Accountant ensure strict compliance with IPSAS No. 23 and the NGAS Manual by timely setting up Real Property Tax Receivables and Deferred Real Property Tax Income at the beginning of each year, in order to uphold the reliability of financial records and the proper implementation of prescribed accounting standards.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Adopt policies and internal controls to monitor loan accommodations and other payroll deductions such that the monthly net take home pay of any personnel of the Municipality does not fall below the mandated minimum amount of ₱5,000.00; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of the succeeding GAAs on the amount of mandatory net take home pay.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant use the SSMI as basis for JEV preparation to recognize the issuances of accountable forms expenses, and maintain SLC for accountable forms; and
The General Services Officer – Designate summarize the RIS, prepare and submit the SSMI to the Accountant to serve as basis in accounting for the issuances of accountable forms, and maintain SC for accountable forms.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Monitor and demand timely submission of liquidation reports and/or immediate refund of any unused balances from accountable officers as soon as the purpose of the cash advance has been served; and
Submit the quarterly reports on the status of unliquidated cash advances within the reglementary period as required under PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer:
Timely submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly project status reports of various government PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that the responsible offices timely submit their GAD-related accomplishment reports to the GFPS Technical Working Group to enable the prompt consolidation and submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Utilize the five percent residual value in the computation of the depreciation expense in accordance with PAG No. 6 of IPSAS No. 17;

Review the computation of depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation for all depreciable PPE accounts following the prescribed procedures provided by IPSAS No. 17; and

Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Management ensure that all necessary documents and required certifications for procurement be prepared and submitted in adherence to Section 86 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
We recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor copies of perfected contracts, together with its complete supporting documents, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management fill up the two vacant mandatory positions under the LDRRMO and provide sufficient funds in order to optimize the performance of the primary functions of the LDRRMO and strengthen the capacity of the Municipality for disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Implement alternative procedures to gather relevant information and evidence to support the reported prior years’ balances such as, but not limited to, obtaining third-party confirmations in order to reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers;

Provide appropriate disclosures in the financial statements regarding the limitations and uncertainties associated with the prior years’ balances; and


Make the necessary correcting entries to accurately reflect the receipt of fund transfer from the National Housing Authority.

We recommended that the Management fill up the two vacant mandatory positions under the LDRRMO and provide sufficient funds in order to optimize the performance of the primary functions of the LDRRMO and strengthen the capacity of the Municipality for disaster risk reduction and management.
We recommended that the Municipal Assessor’s Office follow-up the titling of all parcels of land of the Municipal Government;

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the requirements on the grant and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with existing rules and regulations.  Send demand letters to officials and employees requiring liquidation of their outstanding cash advances. If liquidation was not made despite several demand letters sent, management may consider legal action as one of the options in collecting the unliquidated cash advance.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Utilize the five percent residual value in the computation of the depreciation expense in accordance with PAG No. 6 of IPSAS No. 17;
Review the computation of depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation for all depreciable PPE accounts following the prescribed procedures provided by IPSAS No. 17; and
Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Management ensure that all necessary documents and required certifications for procurement be prepared and submitted in adherence to Section 86 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Management furnish the Auditor copies of perfected contracts, together with its complete supporting documents, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Management fill up the two vacant mandatory positions under the LDRRMO and provide sufficient funds in order to optimize the performance of the primary functions of the LDRRMO and strengthen the capacity of the Municipality for disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Monitor and demand timely submission of liquidation reports and/or immediate refund of any unused balances from accountable officers as soon as the purpose of the cash advance has been served; and
Submit the quarterly reports on the status of unliquidated cash advances within the reglementary period as required under PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to timely submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly project status reports of various government PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which will serve as basis of the latter for the booking up of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts; and

The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances per master list of RPT delinquencies with RPT and SET Receivable account balances per books of accounts to come up with a more accurate and proper balance of RPT and SET Receivable accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following adjusting entries to accurately reflect the balances of the PPE accounts and government equity in the financial statements:

  Particulars	                   Debit    Credit

 Prior Period 
Adjustment	                    xxx                                           
 Accumulated 
Depreciation                                    xxx
 Accumulated 
Impairment Loss 
(if applicable)	xxx
        Appropriate PPE 
        Account	                xxx
            To adjust for issued semi-expendable properties

  Government Equity         xxx
        Prior Period 
        Adjustment                              xxx
            To close the Prior Period Adjustment account

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entries to accurately reflect the nature of the transaction:

 Particulars                Debit         Credit

 Other Prepayments     xxx
      ICT Equipment                      xxx
            To correct entry made in recognizing the asset

 Other MOOE                 xxx
      OtherPrepayments                   xxx
           To recognize the expense portion of the prepayment

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Health Officer furnish copies of documents and reports pertaining to the receipt and utilization of COVID-19 vaccines to the Municipal Accountant;

The Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the receipt and utilization of COVID-19 vaccines by making the following entries:

Particulars                Debit           Credit
Drugs and 
Medicines Inventory   xxx
      Grants and Donations 
       in Kind	                                  xxx
        To take up receipt of vaccines

Drugs and Medicines 
Expenses 
(if pertains to CY)       xxx
Prior Period 
Adjustments 
(if pertains to PY)        xxx
            Drugs and 
           Medicines Inventory           xxx
          To take up utilization of vaccines

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Health Officer coordinate with the Provincial Health Office and the Department of Health to obtain the cost or fair value of the vaccines received by the Municipality.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Health Officer refer to the DPRI of the DOH when setting up the unit prices of essential drugs and medicines in the Purchase Requests (PR) to ensure that reasonable prices are obtained by the Municipality; and

The BAC Chairperson review the submitted PRs for the procurement of essential drugs and medicines to ensure that the DPRI is used in setting up the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC).

We reiterated our recommendation that the Acting Municipal Treasurer ensure the timely submission of the CRAAF and require all collectors and other accountable officers to prepare and timely submit the monthly RAAF for proper monitoring in accordance with Sections 50 and 51 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to submit and observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations.

We recommended the following:

The Municipality’s GFPS, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and

The Local Chief Executive ensure inclusion to their succeeding appropriations necessary expenditures, such as manpower requirements, operating expenses and information technology equipment, for the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to submit and observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant submit the BRS within 10 days after the end of the month as prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-011 to facilitate the timely verification of the balance of the cash in bank accounts of the Municipality and the timely communication and correction of any errors detected.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer ensure the timely submission of the CRAAF and require all collectors and other accountable officers to prepare and timely submit the monthly RAAF for proper monitoring in accordance with Sections 50 and 51 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Bids and Awards Committee furnish the Auditor copies of perfected contract/POs and their supporting documents not later than five working days from perfection of contracts and issuance of POs, together with duly accomplished supporting documents, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and Municipal Accountant to enforce and monitor compliance with the reporting requirement on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Management:
Maximize utilization of the LDRRMF for disaster-related projects and activities and fast-track their implementation following timetables set in the LDRRMF Investment Plan;
Utilize the LDRRMF solely for DRRM-related projects and activities provided in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73; and
Appropriate a separate budget for the operations of the MDRRMO from where administrative expenditures for personnel services, maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay be charged.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Engineer properly monitor and evaluate the implementation of infrastructure projects of the Municipality in accordance to Section 477 (b3) of RA No. 7160; and
The Management strictly abide by the provisions set forth under Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 for the implementation of infrastructure projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to submit the required reports with their supporting documents as prescribed under Sections 1.a.2 and 1.b.1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 20, 2020 and DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipality’s GFPS, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and
The Local Chief Executive ensure inclusion to their succeeding appropriations necessary expenditures, such as manpower requirements, operating expenses and information technology equipment, for the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all PPE at least once a year, submit the RPCPPE in its prescribed form with complete details of all properties and coordinate closely with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of results of the RPCPPE with the accounting records;

The Municipal Accountant reconcile the net difference of ₱7.912 million between the accounting records and property records in accordance with the policies stipulated in the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and

The General Services Officer – Designate create PCs per category of PPE indicating therein the relevant information for proper monitoring.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Exercise due care in recording financial transactions to avert recurrence of similar errors; and

Prepare the following correcting journal entries to correct the errors:

For grants credited to Due to NGA’s

Due to NGA’s xxx
Subsidy from National 
Government   xxx
          To correct entries  
            made for receipt 
               of grant.

For expenses debited to Subsidy from National Government

Specific Expense Account               xxx
Subsidy from National 
Government         xxx
To correct entries 
made to record 
expenses 
charged to 
the grant

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to adopt the Perpetual Inventory System for the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials as well as restore the cost of the undistributed/unconsumed items back to the respective Inventories account at year-end as prescribed by Sections 182, 188 and 190 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and

Direct the General Services Officer – Designate to summarize the RIS, prepare the RSMI monthly and submit to the Municipal Accountant to serve as basis in accounting for the issuances of supplies and materials in accordance with Section 189 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/ schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) and Municipal Accountant to enforce and monitor compliance with the reporting requirement on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and 



The MDRRMO prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP in its prescribed form pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of the same Circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the RCI together with the DVs and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; and

Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to ensure correctness of the RCI and for the timely submission report to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Submit the BRS within 10 days after the end of the month as prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-011, to facilitate the timely verification of the balance of the cash in bank accounts of the Municipality and the timely communication and correction of any errors detected; and

Utilize the online facility of the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) for faster generation of the bank statements.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer require all accountable officers to prepare and timely submit the monthly RAAF for proper monitoring in accordance with Section 51 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and


The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Acting Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly project status reports of various government PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended the following:

The Municipality’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and

The Local Chief Executive ensure inclusion to their succeeding appropriations necessary expenditures, such as manpower requirements, operating expenses and information technology equipment, for the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Require the municipal officials and employees to support the claims for transportation expenses for taxi and other public utility vehicles with official receipts and/or prepare certification for expenses amounting to ₱300.00 or less in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2017-001.

Instruct the municipal officials and employees to follow the provisions of EO No. 77, s. 2019 in the preparation of travel claims.

We recommended that Management adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and furnish the Auditor’s Office, copies of contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting documents within the prescribed period of five (5) working days from issuance thereof. However, indicate all the necessary information in the contracts and POs pertaining to the procurement to facilitate review, evaluation process and post audit of transactions, for transparency and for timely and relevant audit results.
We recommended that the Municipal Assessor’s Office follow-up the titling of all parcels of land of the Municipal Government;

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the requirements on the grant and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with existing rules and regulations.  Send demand letters to officials and employees requiring liquidation of their outstanding cash advances. If liquidation was not made despite several demand letters sent, management may consider legal action as one of the options in collecting the unliquidated cash advance.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all PPE at least once a year, submit the RPCPPE in its prescribed form with complete details of all properties and coordinate closely with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of results of the RPCPPE with the accounting records;
The Municipal Accountant reconcile the net difference of ₱7.912 million between the accounting records and property records in accordance with the policies stipulated in the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and
The General Services Officer – Designate create PCs per category of PPE indicating therein the relevant information for proper monitoring.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Exercise due care in recording financial transactions to avert recurrence of similar errors; and
Prepare the following correcting journal entries to correct the errors:
For grants credited to Due to NGA’s
Due to NGA’s 					xxx
Subsidy from National Government		 xxx
To correct entries made for receipt of grant.
For expenses debited to Subsidy from National Government
Specific Expense Account			xxx
Subsidy from National Government		 xxx
To correct entries made to record expenses
charged to the grant

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to adopt the Perpetual Inventory System for the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials as well as restore the cost of the undistributed/unconsumed items back to the respective Inventories account at year-end as prescribed by Sections 182, 188 and 190 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and
Direct the General Services Officer – Designate to summarize the RIS, prepare the RSMI monthly and submit to the Municipal Accountant to serve as basis in accounting for the issuances of supplies and materials in accordance with Section 189 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) and Municipal Accountant to enforce and monitor compliance with the reporting requirement on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and
The MDRRMO prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP in its prescribed form pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of the same Circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the RCI together with the DVs and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; and
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to ensure correctness of the RCI and for the timely submission report to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Submit the BRS within 10 days after the end of the month as prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-011, to facilitate the timely verification of the balance of the cash in bank accounts of the Municipality and the timely communication and correction of any errors detected; and
Utilize the online facility of the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) for faster generation of the bank statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Acting Municipal Treasurer require all accountable officers to prepare and timely submit the monthly RAAF for proper monitoring in accordance with Section 51 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Acting Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly project status reports of various government PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipality’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and
The Local Chief Executive ensure inclusion to their succeeding appropriations necessary expenditures, such as manpower requirements, operating expenses and information technology equipment, for the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Require the municipal officials and employees to support the claims for transportation expenses for taxi and other public utility vehicles with official receipts and/or prepare certification for expenses amounting to ₱300.00 or less in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2017-001.
Instruct the municipal officials and employees to follow the provisions of EO No. 77, s. 2019 in the preparation of travel claims.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Inventory Committee to:

Conduct annual physical count of PPE;

Prepare a Report on Physical Count of PPE with complete data; and

Instruct the General Services Office (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards and subsidiary ledger for PPE respectively, and ensure that records are reconciled.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that:

The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and

The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that Management:

Thoroughly evaluate the proposed projects and see to it that all factors are considered prior to approval in order to minimize delays, reprogramming and realignment of funds;

Carefully plan their projects to ensure that what is planned and targeted are achievable and/or implementable during the calendar year; and

Strictly monitor the implementation of projects and see to it that projects are completed within the targeted completion date.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer: 

Submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004;

Improve his monitoring and supervision functions and ensure that contractors put up billboards in project sites containing accurate data as soon as the project has been awarded and diligently update the information on the billboard of all projects in order to enhance transparency in project implementation; and


Deduct the cost of billboards from amount due to contractors for the projects without billboards.

We recommended that Management adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and furnish the Auditor’s Office, copies of contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting documents within the prescribed period of five (5) working days from issuance thereof. However, indicate all the necessary information in the contracts and POs pertaining to the procurement to facilitate review, evaluation process and post audit of transactions, for transparency and for timely and relevant audit results.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management:

Adhere to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 in the filling-up of permanent personnel in the MDRRMO; and

Allocate sufficient budget so that the Municipality can appoint permanent personnel in the MDRRMO.
We recommended that management adhere to the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9003 on the operation of controlled dumpsites to ensure the attainment of objectives on ecological solid waste management.
We recommended the that the Municipal Assessor’s Office follow-up the titling of all parcels of land of the Municipal Government;

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the requirements on the grant and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with existing rules and regulations.  Send demand letters to officials and employees requiring liquidation of their outstanding cash advances. If liquidation was not made despite several demand letters sent, management may consider legal action as one of the options in collecting the unliquidated cash advance.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM Officer to prepare and submit each month a Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team of the Municipality on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and

The LDRRMO actively participate in the management of the LDRRMF through efficient delivery of social services to stakeholders/ beneficiaries, and regular submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for updates and monitoring purposes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require strict implementation the provision of COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 particularly on the preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, for audit examination and evaluation.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly for control and monitoring purposes.

We recommended that Management:

Adhere to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 in the filling-up of permanent personnel in the MDRRMO; and

Allocate sufficient budget so that the Municipality can appoint permanent personnel in the MDRRMO.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Inventory Committee to:
Conduct annual physical count of PPE;
Prepare a Report on Physical Count of PPE with complete data; and
Instruct the General Services Office (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards and subsidiary ledger for PPE respectively, and ensure that records are reconciled.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that:
The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and
The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that Management:
Thoroughly evaluate the proposed projects and see to it that all factors are considered prior to approval in order to minimize delays, reprogramming and realignment of funds;
Carefully plan their projects to ensure that what is planned and targeted are achievable and/or implementable during the calendar year; and
Strictly monitor the implementation of projects and see to it that projects are completed within the targeted completion date.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
Submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004;
Improve his monitoring and supervision functions and ensure that contractors put up billboards in project sites containing accurate data as soon as the project has been awarded and diligently update the information on the billboard of all projects in order to enhance transparency in project implementation; and
Deduct the cost of billboards from amount due to contractors for the projects without billboards.

We recommended that Management adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and furnish the Auditor’s Office, copies of contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting documents within the prescribed period of five (5) working days from issuance thereof. However, indicate all the necessary information in the contracts and POs pertaining to the procurement to facilitate review, evaluation process and post audit of transactions, for transparency and for timely and relevant audit results.

We reiterate our previous recommendation that Management:
Adhere to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 in the filling-up of permanent personnel in the MDRRMO; and
Allocate sufficient budget so that the Municipality can appoint permanent personnel in the MDRRMO.
We recommended that management adhere to the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9003 on the operation of controlled dumpsites to ensure the attainment of objectives on ecological solid waste management.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that:

The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and

The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that Management:

Thoroughly evaluate the proposed projects and see to it that all factors are considered prior to approval in order to minimize delays, reprogramming and realignment of funds;

Carefully plan their projects to ensure that what is planned and targeted are achievable and/or implementable during the calendar year; and

Strictly monitor the implementation of projects and see to it that projects are completed within the targeted completion date.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer require contractors/ suppliers to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 pertaining to the posting of relevant project information on tarpaulin signboards at the project location as soon as the award has been made.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Engineer make sure that the government has legal ownership of the land prior to the implementation of municipal projects.  No construction should be made unless ownership of property is secured by the government; and

The Municipal Accountant create safeguards to ensure that government funds are spent only on projects where the government has legal ownership.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management hasten the revision of the 10-year Municipal SWMP and submit to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval to ensure that the SWM programs and activities are planned and implemented with efficiency and effectiveness and aligned with the national government goals.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the requirements on the grant and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with existing rules and regulations.  Send demand letters to officials and employees requiring liquidation of their outstanding cash advances. If liquidation was not made despite several demand letters sent, management may consider legal action as one of the options in collecting the unliquidated cash advance.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM Officer to prepare and submit each month a Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team of the Municipality on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and

The LDRRMO actively participate in the management of the LDRRMF through efficient delivery of social services to stakeholders/ beneficiaries, and regular submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for updates and monitoring purposes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require strict implementation the provision of COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 particularly on the preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, for audit examination and evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly for control and monitoring purposes. 

Further, we recommended that constant follow up of bank statements be made with the depository bank for prompt preparation of BRS.

We recommended that the agency prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report in order to Identify the existence and valuation of the unserviceable property and reclassify them to appropriate account in accordance with the generally accepted accounting principles. 

Furthermore, the municipality should cause the immediate disposal of the unserviceable assets in a mode that is deemed most advantageous to the local government unit.
observations.managementAction: Unserviceable Properties still included in PPE Account.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in CY 2018 AAR Finding No. 3

Finding First Raised in CY 2011 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendations that:
The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and
The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that Management:
Thoroughly evaluate the proposed projects and see to it that all factors are considered prior to approval in order to minimize delays, reprogramming and realignment of funds;
Carefully plan their projects to ensure that what is planned and targeted are achievable and/or implementable during the calendar year; and
Strictly monitor the implementation of projects and see to it that projects are completed within the targeted completion date.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer require contractors/suppliers to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 pertaining to the posting of relevant project information on tarpaulin signboards at the project location as soon as the award has been made.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Engineer make sure that the government has legal ownership of the land prior to the implementation of municipal projects.  No construction should be made unless ownership of property is secured by the government; and
The Municipal Accountant create safeguards to ensure that government funds are spent only on projects where the government has legal ownership.

We recommended that Management:
Adhere to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 in the filling-up of permanent personnel in the MDRRMO; and
Allocate sufficient budget so that the Municipality can appoint permanent personnel in the MDRRMO.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management hasten the revision of the 10-year Municipal SWMP and submit to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval to ensure that the SWM programs and activities are planned and implemented with efficiency and effectiveness and aligned with the national government goals.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive (LCE) form a Committee to conduct annual inventory of all PPE and a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment shall be prepared and submitted to the Auditor thereafter;

The GSO maintain property cards for all types of properties of the LGU, and reconcile balances with the records of the accounting unit; and

The GSO should identify the unserviceable properties and prepare a report that could be used by the Accountant as a basis for dropping the items from the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: RCPPE was submitted but reconciliation of records between the GSO and Accounting Office still ongoing.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in CY 2018 AAR Finding No. 1

Finding was first raised in AAR CY 2013

We recommend that the management adhere to the provisions of NDRRMC DILG DBM CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 particularly on the creation of an Office together with its mandatory positions.

We reiterate our previous years' audit recommendation that the Honorable Mayor create the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete count of the Municipality's PPE and to prepare and submit the report to the Accountant and Auditor concerned. 

Thereafter, the reconciliation of the accounting and the property records should be undertaken.

We recommended that  the Local Chief Executive require strict implementation the provision of COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26,1977 particularly on the  preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, for audit examination and evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly for control and monitoring purposes. Further, we recommended that constant follow up of bank statements be made with the depository bank for prompt preparation of BRS.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers observe the deadlines for the submission of required accounting and financial reports set forth by existing rules and regulations and implement adequate measures that will enable the Agency to meet such deadlines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly enforce and monitor the preparation and submission by the Municipal School Board of the SEF annual budget for the benefit of all public elementary and secondary schools in the locality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly enforce and monitor compliance by the MDRRMO with the reporting requirement on the sources and monitor the utilization of MDRRMF pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended that the GAD Focal Person monitor strictly that activities are implemented on the targeted period so that expected benefits due the beneficiaries will be delivered.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive oversee the prompt implementation of 20% development projects in accordance with the planned schedule to avoid the rising cost of materials and labor.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her collections as well as all collections turned over to her by the collectors/tellers with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day. The Treasurer shall record all deposits made in the cashbook and prepare the RCD.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the affected account balances.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board endeavor to implement strictly programs and projects formulated in the Ten-year Solid Waste Management Plan.  The Local Chief Executive strictly monitor compliance to the Plan pursuant to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the LGU Bids and Awards Committee strictly observe the procurement procedures in accordance with R.A. 9184 and its revised IRR.
We recommended that the local sanggunian update its existing internal rules of procedures and prescribe performance standards of its members, which serve as basis for the computation of RATA.

We recommend that the inventory committee conduct the physical inventory of property and equipment owned by the LGU and prepare RPCPPE to be used as basis for booking up and reconciliation of PPE accounts. After reconciliation the Municipal Accountant effect necessary adjustments if any in the books of accounts for fairness of presentation of the PPE account balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Finding was first raised in CY 2015 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer of Barbaza, Antique require collecting Officers to prepare the Report of Collection and Deposits at the close f each business day and to remit their collections on a daily basis. To enforce this policy we recommend that the Local Chief Executive issue an executive order to this effect.

 We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer should conduct joint review of the accounting policies and procedures to strengthen controls with regards to collections and Deposits.
observations.managementAction: Finding was first raised in CY 2015 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the contracts of service be prepared for all job orders to bind them to perform the service they are hired for in observance of Section 4 (6) of PD 1445.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the liquidation of these outstanding cash advances and the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor liquidation thereof.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit a schedule bearing the names of landowners with areas and assessed value of the lands they own and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the certified list of taxpayers with the corresponding amounts due to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of every calendar year.
We recommend that projects under the 20% Development Plan be implemented on the targeted period so that expected benefits due to beneficiaries/constituents can uplift their living conditions.
We recommend that appropriation for GAD Program be increased to five (5) percent of the LGU’s total appropriation and all targets embodied in the Annual GAD Plan be implemented.

We recommend that the management of LGU – Barbaza revisit the COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 otherwise known as the “Updated Guidelines for the Prevention and Disallowance of Irregular, Unnecessary, Excessive, Extravagant and Unconscionable Expenditures” and require the persons responsible for the return/refund of the excessive amount of the projects to the LGU.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive refund the unutilized cash advance for Confidential/Intelligence Fund and the Municipal Accountant to monitor the refund. Liquidation documents be immediately sent to Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Unit (IFCAU) for audit.

We reiterate our previous years' audit recommendation that the Honorable Mayor create the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete count of the Municipality's PPE and to prepare and submit the report to the Accountant and Auditor concerned. Thereafter, the reconciliation of the accounting and the property records should be undertaken.
observations.reason:
No Motor pool for the safe storage of PPE
Non-conduct of Year end inventory

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive liquidate all her cash advances and direct the employee concerned to settle her cash advances. Likewise, concerned Department Heads should strictly monitor and cause the liquidation of long standing cash advances of their staff to preclude loss or misuse of government funds. The accountable officers involved should be warned that failure to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute valid cause for the withholding of their salaries and allowances.

We recommend that the agency prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report in order to Identify the existence and valuation of the unserviceable property and reclassify them to appropriate account in accordance with the generally accepted accounting principles. Furthermore, the municipality should cause the immediate disposal of the unserviceable assets in a mode that is deemed most advantageous to the local government unit.
